channel.title: Home on Muhammed Adeyemi Adedigba
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Muhammed Adeyemi Adedigba

item.title: About
item.description:
About Me

I am Muhammed Adeyemi Adedigba, an engineering professional with a passion for innovative software solutions that benefit the AECO industry, especially in relation to enabling engineering simulation at scale.

I have an excellent background in computational mechanics, static/dynamic finite element structural analysis, engineering design, and a strong zeal for software development. My formal engineering education culminates with a joint master’s degree in Advanced Solid Mechanics, coordinated by engineering schools and industrial partners across France, Greece, and Italy. I hold a Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering (Structural Engineering specialization).

item.title: Blog
item.description:
Blog posts coming soon...

I will be sharing my ideas, tutorials, and experiences in this section in the future. Stay tuned.

item.title: Projects
item.description:
Explore my profile on GitHub for PyAnsys related activities. Below are highlights of some of the personal finite element analysis related projects I have worked on in the past.

OWT Jacket Design Software Python-based conceptual design software for offshore wind turbine jacket foundations. Interfaces with SACS for automated finite element analysis and structural design. Offshore Engineering SACS Python FEA Link to project to be added soon
General 2D and 3D Finite Element Analysis MATLAB code supporting multiple analysis and element types including T3, Q4, H8, MZC, and QBD elements for a wide range of structural problems. MATLAB FEA Link to project to be added soon
Linear Dynamic 3D FE Analysis of Building Frames 3D linear dynamic FEA code in MAPLE using Euler-Bernoulli beam elements. Computes mode shapes, frequencies, and transient/steady-state response of spatial frames. MAPLE FEA Link to project to be added soon
Stochastic FEA with PCA-accelerated Monte Carlo MATLAB code that obtains the PDF of a system's response under stochastic loads and material properties via Monte Carlo simulation accelerated by PCA. MATLAB Stochastic FEA Link to project to be added soon

item.title: Resume
item.description:
Download CV (PDF)

Education

Joint Master's Degree in Advanced Solid Mechanics Sep 2022 – Aug 2024
Specialisation: Mechanics of Structures Europe
  • Schools: École Centrale de Lille and Université de Lille, France; National Technical University of Athens, Greece; and Università della Calabria, Italy (website: strains.eu)
  • Grades: 16.41/20 (FR); 9.2/10 (GR); 29.50/30 (IT); 18.00/20 (FR) — Ranked 1st on study track (Transcript)
Bachelor of Engineering in Civil Engineering Oct 2015 – Aug 2021
Specialisation: Structural Engineering Nigeria
  • School: University of Ilorin (website: unilorin.edu.ng)
  • Grade: 4.90/5.00 GPA (First Class); Best Graduating Engineering Student — Ranked 1st/774 (Transcript)

Work Experience

Ansys (Part of Synopsys) Oct 2024 – Present
Research and Development Engineer Lyon, France
  • Ownership of the full development lifecycle of Ansys actions, enabling composable CI/CD workflows that automate the development, testing, security hardening, documentation, and release of Ansys OSS.
  • Maintaining simulation data processing libraries (PyDPF-Core/Post) through API contributions, feature enhancements, project documentation overhaul, containerization, and adoption of best practices.
  • Authored PyAnsys Infrastructure as Code, leveraging Terraform for ensuring scalable, secure, and automated management of PyAnsys resources across Azure and GitHub.
  • General PyAnsys ecosystem support, including technical reviews of Ansys open-source repositories; contributions to and support of multiple PyAnsys libraries; and participation in major Python conferences to promote Ansys’s commitment to open-source projects.
  • Authored PyAnsys Logo Generator for standardizing the look and feel of logos for PyAnsys libraries as well as automating their generation and distribution.
INNOSEA (ABL Group) Mar 2024 – Aug 2024
Offshore Structural Engineering Intern Nantes, France
  • Developed a Python-based conceptual design software for offshore wind turbine jacket foundations, which interfaces with SACS through Python APIs for finite element analysis and structural design.
  • Conducted training for internal employees on using completed modules of the conceptual design software while continuously receiving their feedback for improvement opportunities.
  • Gained more competence in using Ansys MAPDL and SACS for stress and fatigue life assessment of offshore structures across conceptual, pre-FEED, FEED, and detailed engineering design phases.
  • Investigated the codebase of PREDIN (a legacy in-house software) for technical documentation preparation to foster proper understanding of legacy code within the team.
Trilogy Sep 2021 – Jul 2022
Software Support Engineer Austin TX, USA (Remote)
  • Utilized programming and computer networking skills, with deep product knowledge and excellent troubleshooting to triage customer problems and provide technical solutions across multiple products.
  • Triaged end-user issues under 15 mins SLA and quality-checked triaged problems with attention to detail, cutting average response time by more than 65%.
  • Demonstrated effective communication skills by engaging clients in meetings for root cause analysis of technical software problems, achieving more than 95% client satisfaction.
  • Facilitated efficient collaboration by coordinating interactions between stakeholders (product managers, internal users, and external customers) and the Development Team.
Hash Analytics Jun 2020 – Jul 2020
Data Analyst Remote
  • Utilized scientific Python libraries (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Seaborn, Scikit-learn) for data scraping, processing, visualisation, and detailed data analysis.
  • Developed a statistical machine learning AI model for predicting employee attrition at companies based on historical data using Scikit-learn.
Chevron Mar 2019 – Aug 2019
Project and Structural Engineering Intern Lagos, Nigeria
  • Gained understanding of using SACS for FE analysis and design of offshore jacket structures.
  • Gathered data points used to enrich the database of an in-house project cost estimation software.
  • Developed a project schedule reporting system which improved project progress reporting time.
  • Provided project control support for various Project Teams, contributing to 100% cost recovery with the JV partner and receiving the 2019 Chevron Human Energy Award for the achievement.

Technical Skills

  • Programming Languages: Python, Java, JavaScript, C, MATLAB, Maple, LaTeX
  • Finite Element Analysis: Ansys (Mechanical, MAPDL), Abaqus, Gmsh, SAP2000
  • Structural Design and CAD: SACS, ProtaStructure, Midas Gen, AutoCAD, Onshape
  • Software Tools: Git, GitHub, Azure, Docker, Linux, Terraform

Research Experience

Development of a Conceptual Design Software for OWT Jackets — Master’s Thesis
